task_completion.description: A Level II window is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window specifically identified as 'Level II' is visible on the screen. This is typically opened via Control Center > New > Level II.

task_completion.description: The 'SPY' instrument is selected in the Level II window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Level II window displays 'SPY' in its instrument selector dropdown or title bar.

task_completion.description: The Order Entry panel is visible within the Level II window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Order Entry interface (e.g., Buy/Sell buttons, order type dropdowns, quantity fields) is visible directly inside the Level II window.

task_completion.description: The Workspaces menu is accessed to save the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Workspaces' menu in the NinjaTrader Control Center is clicked and open.

task_completion.description: The workspace is saved to persist the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save' option is clicked for the active workspace in the Workspaces menu, or a 'Save Workspace As' dialog is confirmed.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the NinjaTrader 8 GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Windows cursor interactions, open dropdown menus, and native NinjaTrader 8 UI elements rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual NinjaTrader 8 application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Control Center and Level II windows match the authentic NinjaTrader 8 interface, not a mocked up image or a different trading platform.

integrity.description: The agent did not artificially paste an Order Entry panel onto the Level II window.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no visual artifacts, mismatched fonts, or overlapping irregular boxes that would suggest image manipulation to fake the Order Entry panel.

privileged_info_for_vlm: SPY is a valid instrument in NinjaTrader 8. A Level II window is opened via Control Center > New > Level II. The default workspace path is C:\Users\<username>\Documents\NinjaTrader 8\workspaces. CRITICAL NOTE: According to official NinjaTrader 8 documentation, Level II windows do NOT have an Order Entry panel (they are strictly for market depth display). Order entry is typically handled by SuperDOM or Basic Entry. The VLM should strictly check if an Order Entry panel is visible in the Level II window as requested by the task, but be aware it natively does not exist, so the agent may fail this specific step.
